jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer, Infrastructure Specialist
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Enterprise Deployment Specialist to lead the implementation and deployment of our secure AI platform, North, within enterprise organizations' environments. This role is crucial in ensuring a seamless and impactful integration of North, enabling our clients to leverage its capabilities effectively.

Responsibilities:

  • Lead end-to-end deployment of North in private cloud and on-premises environments, including planning, configuration, testing, and rollout.

  • Partner with enterprise IT teams to assess infrastructure, security requirements, and data management practices.

  • Design and implement deployment strategies tailored to client needs, ensuring compliance with data privacy and security standards.

  • Troubleshoot and resolve deployment-related technical issues, providing timely solutions to minimize downtime.

  • Develop training materials and conduct sessions to empower users to adopt North’s capabilities effectively.

Requirements:

  • 3+ years of experience deploying enterprise software in private/hybrid cloud environments.

  • You are fluent in both English and Arabic.

  • Strong expertise in cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS, GCP), networking, and virtualization.

  • Proven experience administering production Kubernetes clusters and expertise with Helm.

  • Familiarity with DevOps practices, CI/CD pipelines, and tools like Git for version control.

  • Excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams.

We are open to candidates currently based in the Middle East or who are open to travelling or relocating.

The ideal candidate will have a track record of successful enterprise-level deployments, demonstrating the ability to manage complex projects and deliver impactful results. They should be adaptable, detail-oriented, and passionate about leveraging AI to drive organizational success.

Note: This role requires a deep understanding of both AI technologies and enterprise IT infrastructure, ensuring a seamless integration of North into diverse organizational environments.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

The ideal candidate will have a track record of successful enterprise-level deployments, demonstrating the ability to manage complex projects and deliver impactful results. They should be adaptable, detail-oriented, and passionate about leveraging AI to drive organizational success.

Note: This role requires a deep understanding of both AI technologies and enterprise IT infrastructure, ensuring a seamless integration of North into diverse organizational environments.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. jobs.title: Senior Counsel, Commercial Technology
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

The legal team at Cohere is dynamic, fast-paced and cross-functional, supporting all areas of the organization by providing timely, robust and practical legal advice and support. This role offers a unique opportunity to shape the future of enterprise AI and work on some of the most interesting and multi-faceted commercial negotiations globally. This role will partner closely with our go-to-market team, product and compliance teams alongside other cross-functional stakeholders to shape and scale Cohere’s deployment of B2B enterprise AI solutions. The successful candidate will be actively involved in negotiating a variety of complex commercial arrangements.

As Senior Counsel, Commercial Technology, you will:

  • Lead and support Cohere with all commercial technology matters, including structuring, drafting, negotiating and reviewing complex commercial arrangements.

  • Provide mentorship and support to other members of Cohere’s legal team.

  • Engage with enterprise clients, partners, advisors and vendors on legal matters related to commercial contracting.

  • Work with internal stakeholders and leaders across the organization to answer and resolve legal matters across a variety of functions.

  • Build out legal strategy and refine processes related to commercial contracting, customer acquisition, vendor management and strategic partnerships.

  • Oversee and manage legal contracting contract lifecycle management tools, company policies and procedures.

  • Provide legal guidance, advice and education to the organization on commercial risks and opportunities, including new developments in the law.

  • Optimize contracting processes and create operational efficiencies to empower company objectives, drive growth and reduce friction.

You may be a good fit if:

  • You have a minimum of 7+ years in private practice and/or in-house, with a focus on technology contracting with sophisticated enterprises.

  • You have experience with cross-border transactions, negotiating against counterparties in multiple jurisdictions (e.g., Canada, US, UK/EU).

  • You have extensive experience negotiating Master Services Agreements (MSAs), order forms, statements of work (SOWs), and professional services agreements.

  • Experience in public sector and regulated industry contracting will be considered an asset.

  • You have some familiarity with existing and proposed regulations and issues impacting artificial intelligence.

  • You have extensive experience leading legal negotiations on a variety of complex commercial arrangements, ideally with respect to AI issues.

  • You are able to operate independently, have experience supervising and mentoring lawyers, and are comfortable with the fast-paced and evolving nature of new technology and technology startups.

  • You are a strong communicator (both written and oral), collaborator and team player.

  • You are analytical, collaborative, driven and have a passion for new and evolving areas of technology law focusing on AI.

  • You are focused on solutions and have a practical, business-oriented mindset.

  • Experience negotiating contracts with AI issues and complex strategic partnerships or collaborations with enterprises will be considered an asset.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Enablement & Adoption Specialist
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Role Overview

As the Enablement & Adoption Specialist at Cohere, you will own and pioneer the Partner Enablement strategy for our cutting-edge frontier AI enterprise platform. This role is critical in designing and building scalable enablement programs from the ground up, architecting frameworks that empower partners to successfully sell, implement, and support Cohere’s solutions. Reporting directly to the Enablement & Adoption Lead, you will collaborate closely with the broader Enablement & Adoption team and Partner team to align initiatives with partner success outcomes, driving long-term partner growth. This position demands thought leadership, innovation, and a willingness to redefine partner enablement for frontier AI technologies.

Key Responsibilities

  • Pioneer and execute a comprehensive, innovative partner enablement strategy, including scalable onboarding processes, certification frameworks, and continuous learning paths tailored for Cohere solutions.

  • Develop a deep expertise in Cohere’s agent foundry North and Cohere models, with the ability to become a subject matter expert and confidently articulate value propositions, use cases, and technical capabilities to partners and internal stakeholders.

  • Develop tiered, industry-leading certification programs to validate partner expertise across sales, technical domains, setting new standards for partner competency in Cohere solutions and Generative AI.

  • Build next-generation co-selling frameworks (e.g., joint value propositions, partner playbooks) and foster cross-functional partnerships with Go-To-Market (GTM), Product Marketing, Product Management, and Forward Deployed Engineering teams to align enablement content with market positioning, product roadmaps, and technical implementation needs.

  • Design globally scalable enablement blueprints and frameworks that adapt to diverse user profiles, regional markets and partner segments, ensuring consistent competency development worldwide

  • Collaborate with the broader Enablement & Adoption team to distill adoption best practices, customer insights, and training methodologies from Cohere’s internal and direct customer engagements, adapting these learnings into partner-facing enablement strategies. Partner with the Partner Delivery Managers to build and deploy training. Be a thought partner/ advisor to partners to define their Cohere enablement journey.

  • Create and maintain dynamic, engaging live and digital training programs, enablement assets, and certification resources to drive self-service enablement and partner competency, leveraging emerging technologies for delivery.

Required Skills & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in a related field (or equivalent experience); advanced degree preferred.

  • 8+ years of experience in partner/ sales enablement, strategic partnerships, sales engineering, strategic customer success or strategy & operations within high-tech or enterprise software companies.

  • Proven track record of building and scaling innovative enablement programs and certification frameworks from the ground up.

  • Exceptional communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts into clear, engaging educational content for diverse audiences.

  • Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional initiatives and influence stakeholders in a fast-paced, agile environment.

  • Strong technical acumen with familiarity in LLMs and AI platforms, ideally with experience supporting complex, enterprise end-to-end solutions.

  • Ability to travel regionally and globally, when necessary.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Track record of success in fast-paced, high-growth environments

  • Consulting experience at a top-tier firm or in a strategic advisory role, with expertise in GTM strategy, partner ecosystems, or enablement program design.

  • Strong understanding of enterprise software sales processes, partner ecosystem dynamics, and channel strategies.

  • Experience developing adoption programs for both technical and non-technical audiences.

  • Experience with learning management systems (LMS), partner portals, and content curation platforms.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Lead, Government Affairs - APAC
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Cohere is a fast growing company in the extremely dynamic artificial intelligence space. We are building a Government Affairs and Public Policy (GAPP) team that has the drive, and experience to engage in, and shape a nascent and unique policy environment for enterprise AI.

We seek a Government Affairs professional to lead our APAC government affairs strategy. Candidates will have a proven track record, are analytical, collaborative, driven, and possess a passion for the new and evolving public policy debate at the intersection of enterprise technology and artificial intelligence.

There’s no better time to join us in building this function as we shape the future of AI.

As our Lead for Asia Government Affairs you will:

  • Partner closely with Cohere’s senior leadership, legal, public policy and responsible AI teams to develop, coordinate and implement a government affairs and stakeholder engagement strategy to advocate for responsible AI policies and related issues.

  • Expand Cohere’s profile as the go-to thought-leader and industry partner of choice on issues relating to safe and responsible artificial intelligence policy, regulation and enforcement.

  • Guide and coordinate Cohere’s political engagement in our priority markets in APAC by cultivating relationships, and represent the company with key government officials, lawmakers, regulators, and other third-party stakeholders to influence the development of artificial intelligence legislation, regulations and public policy.

  • Work closely with public sector sales teams and in shaping technology policies in regulated sectors (such as banking, healthcare and others).

  • Proactively monitor, report and work with cross functional teams to craft thoughtful responses to governmental initiatives and regulatory issues that may impact Cohere’s business or products.

  • In collaboration with Cohere’s public relations and marketing teams, support effective messaging relating to Cohere’s position on artificial intelligence policy and regulation.

  • Manage government relations consultants and trade associations.

  • Ensure compliance of lobbying and political activities with local laws.

You may be a good fit for this career opportunity, if you have:

  • Broad knowledge of technology policy and artificial intelligence, its application, and uses, and experience in understanding, debating, and shaping relevant policies and regulations in the region.

  • Experience in building and leading a government affairs team, advising, and collaborating with cross-functional internal & external stakeholders.

  • A strong aptitude to navigate intricate and fluid political and policy debates across jurisdictions, prioritize issues and distill key messages for a variety of audiences.

  • A growth mindset, and a self-starter with the ability to work independently, and get things done.

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a proven ability to draft compelling policy documents, thought leadership content, and customer communications.

  • 15+ years of proven leadership experience working with or in government, public interest groups, trade associations or corporate public policy organizations.

  • Bachelor's degree in Law, Public Policy, International Relations, or a related field.

  • Regional (Japan, Korea and/or Singapore) and multilingual experience is a must have.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Data Annotation Specialist, Simplified Chinese / Mandarin Writer
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

We are on a mission to build machines that understand the world and make them safely accessible to all. Data quality is foundational to this process. Machines (or Large Language Models to be exact) learn in similar ways to humans - by way of feedback. By labelling, ranking, auditing, and correcting text output, you will improve Large Language Model’s performance for iterations to come, thus having a lasting impact on Cohere’s tech.


Cohere seeks dynamic and dedicated Data Annotators with exceptional Chinese Writing, Translation, and Data Manipulation skill.

Please Note: This is a part-time independent contractor position available within Canada. We seek candidates who are able to commit to 16 hours per week minimum at a 30 CAD/hour contract rate. This role is BYOD 💻 - Bring Your Own Device (laptop). Remote work within Canada. 12 month contract. Performance incentives included!

As a Data Annotation Specialist, you will:

  • Label and Rank: Accurately label and rank machine learning data with advanced proficiency in Chinese, ensuring data integrity and quality.

  • Audit and Correct: Scrutinize and rectify any inaccuracies in language data (may include text, image, JSON, TSV/CSV, Markdown), maintaining the highest standard of data accuracy.

  • Reading and Text-Based Tasks: Efficiently complete reading and text-based assignments, with high attention to detail.
